title: System Design
description: Generate a system-design outline with phased thinking, core modules, and tradeoffs.
noticeDescription: This version centers on {{scenario}}, targets {{scale}} scale, and prioritizes {{priority}}.

0.title: Define the read/write path
0.description: At {{scale}} scale, clarify the critical path before drawing more boxes.

1.title: Add data and cache layers
1.description: Define hot paths, cache hit strategy, and invalidation rules.

2.title: Add reliability controls
2.description: Cover rate limiting, degradation, retries, monitoring, and isolation.

3.title: Close with an evolution plan
3.description: Clarify what ships first and how the design grows later.

0.title: Write gateway
0.role: Handles content writes, validation, and idempotency.

1.title: Timeline service
1.role: Builds user-specific feed results with paging and ordering.

2.title: Cache layer
2.role: Caches hot home and timeline responses to reduce source pressure.

3.title: Async fan-out
3.role: Processes follower delivery, recommendation refresh, and stats backfill.

0.title: Connection layer
0.role: Maintains connections, auth, and session routing.

1.title: Message storage
1.role: Preserves durability, history replay, and ordering.

2.title: Delivery service
2.role: Handles online delivery, offline replay, and retries.

3.title: State sync
3.role: Synchronizes read state, unread counters, and conversation summaries.

0.title: Ingestion layer
0.role: Receives events and batch imports in a unified path.

2.title: Query service
2.role: Serves low-latency data queries for report dimensions.

3.title: Visualization layer
3.role: Manages chart configuration, export, and permissions.

latency.0: Keep the critical path short and stable, even if some eventual consistency is deferred.
latency.1: Place cache close to the hottest path and be explicit about invalidation.
latency.2: Move fan-out or heavy computation off the request path whenever possible.
latency.3: Track P95 and P99, not only averages.

consistency.0: Define the source of truth first so multi-writer drift does not spread.
consistency.1: Be explicit about ordering, dedupe, and replay semantics.
consistency.2: Cache hit rate is not the primary goal if correctness suffers.
consistency.3: The failure plan must explain how state remains trustworthy.

cost.0: Start simple and expand only where real bottlenecks appear.
cost.1: Avoid introducing high-maintenance components too early.
cost.2: Tiered storage and on-demand compute are often cheaper than keeping everything hot.
cost.3: Make capacity and eviction strategy explicit to prevent cost drift.
